message:
Running command: 
# 45m15s CPU / ~12.3min elapsed. Pipe log stopped at 2061 bytes.
# Pipe might be blocked again. The aggressive drainer with O_NONBLOCK should be helping
# but the log shows the same last messages.

# Check process state
ORPID=1101105
LIBPID=1100961

T1=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
SC=$(cat /proc/$ORPID/syscall 2>/dev/null | head -c5)
echo "OpenROAD T=$T1 SYSCALL=$SC"

sleep 5
T2=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
echo "After 5s: T=$T2"

# Force drain
dd if=/proc/$LIBPID/fd/11 bs=65536 >> /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null &
DRAIN=$!
sleep 3
kill $DRAIN 2>/dev/null
wait $DRAIN 2>/dev/null

echo "After force drain:"
wc -c /tmp/drt2_pipe.log
tail -10 /tmp/drt2_pipe.log

function.name: execute_bash
